title: fragaria chiloensis subsp. sandwicensis
intro: Fragaria chiloensis subsp. sandwicensis, also known as the Hawaiian strawberry, is a subspecies of the wild strawberry plant native to the Pacific coast of North America and the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and improve heart health. The health benefits of consuming Fragaria chiloensis subsp. sandwicensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by using the leaves and flowers of the plant, and are said to have a calming effect on the nervous system.
